findings.title: String-formatted SQL query
findings.description: SQL query constructed via string formatting allows injection.
findings.code: conn.execute("INSERT OR IGNORE INTO users VALUES (1,'admin','" +
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM t WHERE id=?', (id,))

findings.title: String-formatted SQL query
findings.description: SQL query constructed via string formatting allows injection.
findings.code: conn.execute("INSERT OR IGNORE INTO users VALUES (2,'alice','" +
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM t WHERE id=?', (id,))

findings.title: String-formatted SQL query
findings.description: SQL query constructed via string formatting allows injection.
findings.code: rows = conn.execute("SELECT id, username, email, role, password FROM users").fetchall()
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM t WHERE id=?', (id,))

findings.title: subprocess with shell=True
findings.description: shell=True passes command to the OS shell, enabling injection.
findings.remediation: Use a list of arguments and shell=False: subprocess.run(['/bin/cmd', arg])

findings.title: pickle.loads on potentially untrusted data
findings.description: Deserializing untrusted pickle data allows arbitrary code execution.
findings.remediation: Use JSON for data exchange. Never deserialize untrusted binary data with pickle.
findings.owasp: A08:2021 – Software and Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: MD5 used for password hashing
findings.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password storage.
findings.remediation: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2: bcrypt.hashpw(password, bcrypt.gensalt())

findings.title: MD5 used for password hashing
findings.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password storage.
findings.remediation: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2: bcrypt.hashpw(password, bcrypt.gensalt())

findings.title: MD5 used for password hashing
findings.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password storage.
findings.remediation: Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2: bcrypt.hashpw(password, bcrypt.gensalt())

findings.title: Hardcoded secret / password
findings.description: Credentials embedded in source code are exposed in version control.
findings.remediation: Load secrets from environment variables or a secrets manager.

findings.title: Hardcoded secret / password
findings.description: Credentials embedded in source code are exposed in version control.
findings.remediation: Load secrets from environment variables or a secrets manager.

findings.title: Hardcoded secret / password
findings.description: Credentials embedded in source code are exposed in version control.
findings.remediation: Load secrets from environment variables or a secrets manager.

findings.title: Hardcoded secret / password
findings.description: Credentials embedded in source code are exposed in version control.
findings.code: query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='{username}' AND password='{hashed}'"
findings.remediation: Load secrets from environment variables or a secrets manager.

findings.title: Flask debug=True
findings.description: Debug mode exposes a browser-accessible Python REPL — full RCE risk.
findings.remediation: Set debug=False in production. Use FLASK_ENV=production.

findings.title: Flask bound to 0.0.0.0
findings.description: Binding to all interfaces exposes the dev server publicly.
findings.remediation: Bind to 127.0.0.1 and use a production WSGI server (gunicorn/uwsgi) behind a reverse proxy.

findings.title: open() with user-controlled filename
findings.description: User input in file paths allows directory traversal (../../etc/passwd).
findings.remediation: Resolve and validate path is inside a trusted base directory using Path.resolve().

findings.title: Potential sensitive data in log statement
findings.description: Logging credentials creates sensitive data exposure in log files.
findings.remediation: Never log passwords, tokens, or secrets. Log only user IDs and event types.

findings.title: Import of dangerous module
findings.description: These serialization modules are dangerous with untrusted data.
findings.remediation: Prefer json module for data exchange. Use pickle only with trusted local data.
findings.owasp: A08:2021 – Software and Data Integrity Failures
